appraisal_events.evidence: Regards ‘imprégnés d’une telle bonté’ comme à un bébé; pain ‘du genre de ceux qu’on jette aux canards’; ‘la grand-mère n’était plus un canard ou une antilope, mais déjà … un “baby”.’
appraisal_events.explanation: The princess’s benevolence is framed by the narrator as miscalibrated and patronizing, animalizing/infantilizing the grandmother and thus locally lowering her standing.

status_effects.explanation: She is included by a princess but treated as an animal/child, which the narrator ironizes as a condescending lowering.
